mapping.a_element: spin-glass type problems characterized by competition and heterogeneities leading to long epochs of stasis and crises
mapping.b_element: frustrated, disordered systems such as spin glasses in combinatorial optimization

mapping.a_element: long epochs of stasis interrupted by crises due to hyper-sensitivity to small environmental changes
mapping.b_element: ground state properties of frustrated systems determined by combinatorial optimization methods

mapping.a_element: methodological contribution of physics to economics via simple models and agent-based simulations
mapping.b_element: application of combinatorial optimization algorithms (e.g., network flows, minimum-cost-flow) to determine ground states in frustrated systems

brief_justification: Both papers invoke spin-glass systems as a core mechanistic metaphor: Paper A uses spin-glass dynamics (competition, heterogeneities, and phase transitions) to model economic crises, while Paper B explicitly studies spin glasses and other frustrated systems via combinatorial optimization to determine ground states. The shared machinery is the spin-glass framework itself, where disorder and frustration lead to complex, sensitive dynamics or states.
